voltage_ratio.text: Wie hoch ist die Betriebsspannung im Verhältnis zur Nennspannung?

low.label: < 50% der Nennspannung
low.desc: DC-Bias-Derating ist weniger kritisch, aber bei Klasse-II-Dielektrika dennoch relevant

medium.label: 50–80% der Nennspannung
medium.desc: DC-Bias-Derating ist ein erhebliches Problem — zwei identische MLCCs können 30–60% Kapazität verlieren

high.label: > 80% der Nennspannung
high.desc: Starkes DC-Bias-Derating — bei diesem Verhältnis sind nur C0G/NP0-Dielektrika sicher

flex_pcb.text: Wird dieses Bauteil auf einer Flex- oder Starrflex-Leiterplatte montiert?

yes.label: Ja — Flex- oder Starrflex-PCB
yes.desc: Standard-MLCCs brechen unter Platinenbiegung, was zu Kurzschlüssen und potenziellen Bränden führt

no.label: Nein — starre Leiterplatte
no.desc: Flexible Terminierung ist nicht erforderlich, aber akzeptabel wenn vorhanden

audio_path.text: Befindet sich dieses Bauteil in einem Audio- oder Analogsignalpfad?

yes.label: Ja — Audio / Analog
yes.desc: Klasse-II-Dielektrika (X7R, X5R) zeigen piezoelektrische Effekte, die hörbare Geräusche verursachen

no.label: Nein
no.desc: Piezoelektrisches Rauschen ist kein Problem

environment.text: Für welche Umgebung ist dieses Bauteil bestimmt?
Für welche Umgebung ist dieses Bauteil bestimmt?

precision.text: Handelt es sich um eine Präzisions- oder Instrumentierungsanwendung?

yes.label: Ja — Präzision / Instrumentierung
yes.desc: TKR- und Toleranzgrenzen werden verschärft; Dünnschichttechnologie kann erforderlich sein

no.label: Nein — Allzweck
no.desc: Standard-Parameterzuordnung ist ausreichend

environment.text: Für welche Umgebung ist dieses Bauteil bestimmt?

yes.label: Ja — 4-Leiter-Kelvin-Anschluss
yes.desc: Separate Force/Sense-Pads sind für die Eliminierung des Leitungswiderstands erforderlich

no.label: Nein — Standard 2-Leiter
no.desc: Standard-Footprint-Zuordnung ist ausreichend

high.label: Hohe Präzision (<1%)
high.desc: Enge Toleranz, niedriger TKR und niedrige parasitäre Induktivität sind entscheidend

rough.label: Grob (>5%)
rough.desc: Toleranz und TKR sind unkritisch — Überstromerkennnung mit großem Sicherheitsabstand

high_frequency.label: Hohe Frequenz (>100 kHz)
high_frequency.desc: Parasitäre Induktivität wird kritisch — Metallstreifen oder umgekehrte Geometrie bevorzugt

unknown.label: Unbekannt
unknown.desc: Parasitäre Induktivität wird zur Überprüfung markiert

thermal_management.text: Wie wird der Widerstand thermisch verwaltet?

dedicated_heatsink.label: Dedizierter Kühlkörper mit bekanntem Wärmewiderstand
dedicated_heatsink.desc: Wärmewiderstand und Schnittstellenabmessungen sind entscheidend für die Leistungsabgabe

chassis_mounted.label: Gehäusemontiert (Gehäusewand, Metallrahmen)
chassis_mounted.desc: Der Gehäuse-Wärmepfad hat andere Eigenschaften als ein dedizierter Kühlkörper

free_standing.label: Kein Kühlkörper / freistehend
free_standing.desc: Die Nennleistung muss gegenüber der Montage-Nennleistung stark herabgesetzt werden

yes.label: Ja — lüftergekühlt
yes.desc: Die Nennleistung kann die Derating-Kurve für Lüfterkühlung verwenden

no.label: Nein — natürliche Konvektion
no.desc: Die Nennleistung muss das Derating für natürliche Konvektion verwenden — konservativer

precision.text: Handelt es sich um eine Präzisions- oder Instrumentierungsanwendung?

yes.label: Ja — Präzision / Instrumentierung
yes.desc: TKR- und Toleranzgrenzen werden verschärft; Dünnschichttechnologie kann erforderlich sein

no.label: Nein — Allzweck
no.desc: Standard-Parameterzuordnung ist ausreichend

environment.text: Für welche Umgebung ist dieses Bauteil bestimmt?

ripple_frequency.text: Wie hoch ist die Schalt- / Ripplefrequenz?

120hz.label: 120Hz (Netzgleichrichtung)
120hz.desc: Verwenden Sie den Rippelstrom-Wert aus dem Datenblatt direkt

high_frequency.label: Hohe Frequenz (Schaltnetzteil)
high_frequency.desc: Der Rippelstrom steigt bei höheren Frequenzen (1,4–1,7x bei 100kHz gegenüber 120Hz)

unknown.label: Unbekannt
unknown.desc: Verwendet den 120Hz-Basiswert und markiert zur Überprüfung, falls in einem Schaltregler verwendet

ambient_temp.text: Wie hoch ist die tatsächliche Umgebungstemperatur?

unknown.label: Unbekannt
unknown.desc: Lebensdauer kann nicht optimiert werden — die Nennlebensdauer wird als fester Schwellenwert verwendet

polarization.text: Handelt es sich um eine polarisierte oder unpolarisierte Anwendung?

non_polarized.label: Unpolarisiert / Bipolar (AC-Kopplung)
non_polarized.desc: Bipolare/unpolarisierte Elektrolytkondensatoren erforderlich — Umkehrspannung verursacht Gasentwicklung und Belüftung

safety_critical.text: Ist der sicherheitskritische Ausfallmodus ein Problem?

yes.label: Ja — Kurzschluss/Entzündung nicht tolerierbar
yes.desc: MnO2-Typen müssen markiert oder ausgeschlossen werden; Polymer-Typen fallen gutartig aus (offener Stromkreis)

no.label: Nein — ausreichender Schaltungsschutz vorhanden
no.desc: Sowohl MnO2 als auch Polymer sind akzeptabel, aber der Ausfallmodus wird dennoch markiert

voltage_derating.text: Wie hoch ist die Betriebsspannung als Prozentsatz der Nennspannung?

50_percent.label: 50%-Derating-Regel eingehalten
50_percent.desc: Branchenbeste Praxis — Betrieb bei ≤50% der Nennspannung reduziert die Ausfallrate drastisch

above_50.label: Betrieb über 50% der Nennspannung
above_50.desc: Hohes Risiko bei MnO2-Typen — alle Kandidaten markieren

unknown.label: Unbekannt
unknown.desc: Ungünstigsten Fall annehmen — zur Überprüfung markieren

yes.label: Ja — Vorwiderstand oder Sanftanlauf
yes.desc: Stoßstrom wird begrenzt — Standard-Zuordnung für ESR und Spannung

no.label: Nein — hartes Einschalten auf Kondensator
no.desc: Stoßstrom ist unbegrenzt — MnO2-Typen sind besonders gefährdet

120hz.label: 120 Hz (netzgleichgerichtet)
120hz.desc: Rippelstrom-Nennwert bei 120 Hz aus dem Datenblatt gilt direkt

high_freq.label: Spezifische hohe Frequenz (>10 kHz)
high_freq.desc: Rippelstrom-Nennwert variiert mit der Frequenz — Derating überprüfen

unknown.label: Unbekannt
unknown.desc: Rippelstrom-Nennwert bei tatsächlicher Frequenz muss überprüft werden

yes.label: Ja — Polymer wegen ESR gewählt
yes.desc: ESR-Zuordnung wird zwingend — der Hauptgrund für die Wahl von Polymer gegenüber Standard-Elektrolyt

no.label: Nein — Standardauswahl
no.desc: Standard-ESR-Zuordnung ist ausreichend

environment.text: Für welche Umgebung ist dieses Bauteil bestimmt?

function.text: Was ist die Hauptfunktion dieses Superkondensators?

backup.label: Energiepuffer / Überbrückung
backup.desc: RTC, SRAM, Spannungseinbruch-Überbrückung — Leckstrom und Selbstentladung sind primär

pulse.label: Pulslast-Pufferung
pulse.desc: GSM-Bursts, Motorstarts, Rekuperationsbremsung — ESR und Spitzenstrom sind primär

harvesting.label: Energy-Harvesting-Puffer
harvesting.desc: Leckstrom muss niedriger sein als die Harvester-Leistung — Selbstentladung bestimmt die Akkumulation

yes.label: Ja — Automobil, Außenbereich
yes.desc: Tieftemperatur-ESR kann bei -40°C um das 5–10-fache steigen — Derating-Kurve muss überprüft werden

no.label: Nein — Innenbereich / kontrolliert
no.desc: Standard-ESR-Spezifikation ist ausreichend

emi.label: AC mains filtering / EMI suppression
emi.desc: Safety rating (X/Y class) becomes mandatory; AC voltage rating is primary

dc_filtering.label: DC filtering / coupling / bypass
dc_filtering.desc: DC voltage rating is primary; safety rating not required

snubber.label: Snubber / pulse discharge
snubber.desc: dV/dt and peak pulse current are primary — film/foil construction preferred

motor_run.label: Motor-run / power factor correction
motor_run.desc: AC voltage rating and ripple current are primary; must be rated for continuous AC

precision.label: Precision timing / resonant circuit
precision.desc: Dissipation factor, temperature coefficient, and tolerance become primary

unknown.label: Unbekannt
unknown.desc: All candidates will be flagged for dV/dt review — do not substitute without verifying

application_type.text: Is this in an automotive application?

mains.label: AC mains surge protection (lightning, switching)
mains.desc: Safety rating and thermal disconnect become mandatory; energy and surge current are primary

dc_automotive.label: DC bus / automotive protection (load dump, inductive spikes)
dc_automotive.desc: Safety rating not required; max DC voltage and response time are primary

esd.label: ESD / signal-line protection
esd.desc: Low capacitance and fast response time are critical for signal integrity

thermal_disconnect.text: Does the original have a thermal disconnect / fuse?

yes.label: Yes — has thermal disconnect
yes.desc: Replacement MUST also have thermal disconnect — prevents thermal runaway and fire

no.label: No — bare MOV
no.desc: Thermal disconnect not required but may be an upgrade; verify circuit has external overcurrent protection

unknown.label: Unknown — need to inspect
unknown.desc: Must inspect original or circuit design before proceeding

environment.text: Is this in an automotive application?

automotive.label: Ja — Automobil
automotive.desc: AEC-Q200 mandatory; operating temp must cover automotive range; surge ratings for ISO 7637

no.label: No — standard / industrial
no.desc: Standard-Umgebungsabgleich

circuit_voltage.text: What is the maximum circuit voltage?

low.label: Low voltage (≤6V)
low.desc: Initial resistance voltage drop is significant — verify Ihold × R₁ is acceptable

medium.label: Medium voltage (6–60V)
medium.desc: Standard Vmax margin should be verified

high.label: High voltage (>60V)
high.desc: Vmax is the most critical parameter — arcing risk above rating

ambient_temperature.text: What is the ambient operating temperature?

specific_temp.label: Elevated temperature (>40°C)
specific_temp.desc: Hold and trip currents must be derated — PTC fuses are temperature-sensitive

room_temp.label: Room temperature (~25°C)
room_temp.desc: Datasheet ratings apply directly — no derating needed

unknown.label: Unbekannt
unknown.desc: Must determine ambient temperature before finalizing selection

fault_frequency.text: Will the fuse experience frequent trip/reset cycles?

frequent.label: Frequent (part of normal operation)
frequent.desc: Endurance cycles, resistance creep, and initial resistance are critical concerns

function.text: Is the R-T curve encoded in firmware?

sensing.label: NTC — Temperature sensing
sensing.desc: R25, B-value, tolerances, and R-T curve accuracy are primary

inrush.label: NTC — Inrush current limiting
inrush.desc: Cold resistance, max current, max power, and thermal recovery are primary

compensation.label: NTC — Temperature compensation
compensation.desc: R25 and B-value must match the compensation target exactly; drift matters

accuracy.text: What temperature accuracy do you need?

standard.label: Standard (±1-2°C)
standard.desc: R25 tolerance ≤5%, B-value tolerance ≤3% — B-value matching is sufficient

precision.label: Precision (±0.5°C or better)
precision.desc: R25 tolerance ≤1%, B-value tolerance ≤1% — Steinhart-Hart verification required

firmware_rt.text: Is the R-T curve encoded in firmware?

yes.label: Yes — lookup table or Steinhart-Hart in code
yes.desc: Replacement must conform to the same R-T curve or firmware must be updated

no.label: No — analog circuit (voltage divider + comparator)
no.desc: B-value match is sufficient — curve shape at extremes matters less

overcurrent.label: Overcurrent protection (resettable fuse)
overcurrent.desc: Curie/switch temperature, hold current, trip current, and max voltage are primary

heater.label: Self-regulating heater
heater.desc: Curie temperature (equilibrium temp), power rating, and form factor are primary

signal.label: Signal-line
signal.desc: USB, HDMI, Ethernet, CAN, LVDS, MIPI — impedance at frequency is primary

power.label: Power-line
power.desc: AC mains filter, DC bus filter — inductance and rated current are primary

usb2.label: USB 2.0
usb2.desc: 90Ω ±15% impedance — leakage inductance and mode conversion are important

usb3.label: USB 3.x / USB4
usb3.desc: Very high speed — mode conversion (Scd21) is the dominant concern

ethernet.label: 100/1000BASE-T Ethernet
ethernet.desc: Specific insertion loss and return loss specs per IEEE 802.3

can.label: CAN / CAN-FD
can.desc: Lower speed, more tolerant of leakage inductance

yes.label: Yes — AC mains (120V/240V)
yes.desc: Safety rating becomes mandatory; voltage must cover mains + transients

no.label: No — DC bus or low-voltage power
no.desc: Safety rating not required; voltage and current rating are still primary

signal_or_power.text: Is this ferrite bead on a power rail or a signal line?

signal.label: Signal line
signal.desc: Filtering a data or clock signal (e.g., I2C, SPI, clock)

operating_current.text: What is the actual DC operating current (peak)?

unknown.label: Unknown / varies
unknown.desc: Will flag all candidates for DC bias derating review

broadband.label: Broadband / unknown
broadband.desc: Will flag for impedance curve review across full band

circuit_type.text: What type of converter/circuit is this in?

switcher.label: Buck/boost/buck-boost switching converter
switcher.desc: Both Isat and Irms are critical; core material saturation behavior matters

linear.label: LDO output / general filtering
linear.desc: Irms is primary (thermal); Isat is less critical — no switching-induced current peaks

emi.label: EMI filter / common mode
emi.desc: May be using the wrong component type — consider a common mode choke or ferrite bead

operating_current.text: What is the actual operating DC current?

unknown.label: Unbekannt
unknown.desc: Will flag for Isat derating review

yes.label: Yes — shielded inductor required
yes.desc: Unshielded cannot replace shielded

no.label: No / not sure
no.desc: Shielded can always replace unshielded (upgrade)

low_rf.label: Low RF (100 kHz – 30 MHz)
low_rf.desc: Ferrite cores may still be usable; SRF must be well above operating frequency

high_rf.label: High RF / microwave (>30 MHz)
high_rf.desc: Air/ceramic core only; Q factor is the dominant specification

broadband.label: Broadband / wideband
broadband.desc: SRF and flat impedance over wide bandwidth are primary concerns

unknown.label: Unbekannt
unknown.desc: Flag SRF and Q factor for review

high_q.label: High Q (>50)
high_q.desc: Requires air/ceramic core, tight tolerances; shielding may degrade Q

moderate_q.label: Moderate Q (20–50)
moderate_q.desc: Standard Q matching — verify at operating frequency

low_q.label: Low Q / don\
low_q.desc: Q is not a primary selection criterion

yes.label: Yes — shielded required
yes.desc: Shielded inductor required; verify Q impact

no.label: No / don\
no.desc: Unshielded may offer higher Q; shielded can always replace unshielded (upgrade)

sar.label: SAR (Successive-Approximation Register)
sar.desc: Delta-Sigma, Pipeline, and Flash candidates blocked. 1-cycle conversion latency, 8–18 bit resolution, 1 kSPS–5 MSPS. Ideal for control loops, multiplexed multi-channel measurement, and low-latency applications. Low power, no pipeline delay.

delta_sigma.label: Delta-Sigma (Sigma-Delta)
delta_sigma.desc: SAR, Pipeline, and Flash candidates blocked. High resolution (16–32 bit), high latency (decimation filter group delay = (filter_order × decimation_ratio) / ODR, often 10s–100s ms). Ideal for precision DC measurement, weighing, thermocouples, audio. Fatal in fast control loops due to conversion latency.

pipeline.label: Pipeline
pipeline.desc: SAR, Delta-Sigma, and Flash candidates blocked. High throughput (10–500 MSPS) with moderate resolution (8–16 bit). Pipeline latency = several clock cycles. Ideal for communications receivers, imaging, oscilloscopes. Sample rate and THD are the dominant specifications.

flash.label: Flash / Folding-Interpolating
flash.desc: SAR, Delta-Sigma, and Pipeline candidates blocked. Very high speed (>500 MSPS), low resolution (6–10 bit), high power (100+ mW typical), 1-cycle latency. Ideal for extreme-speed applications (high-speed digitizers, oscilloscopes, radar). Not suitable for precision measurement.

precision_class.text: What is the resolution / precision class of the application?

general_12bit.label: ≤12-bit general purpose (sensor digitization, position feedback, general control)
general_12bit.desc: Default thresholds apply. ENOB is informational (Application Review). INL ≤ 2 LSB and DNL ≤ 1 LSB are sufficient. Reference type matching is standard. THD is informational.

precision_16bit.label: 12–16-bit precision (industrial measurement, data acquisition, precision control)
precision_16bit.desc: ENOB, INL, DNL, reference type, and THD all escalated to primary. INL tightened to ≤ 1 LSB, DNL to ≤ 0.5 LSB. Linearity errors at this resolution become significant for closed-loop control accuracy.

high_precision_24bit.label: 16–24-bit high precision (weighing, pressure, instrumentation, audio)
high_precision_24bit.desc: ENOB escalated to mandatory + blockOnMissing. INL and DNL escalated to mandatory (≤ 0.5 LSB). Reference type escalated to mandatory. Reference voltage escalated to primary. At this resolution, linearity errors are catastrophic and reference accuracy dominates system performance.

sampling_topology.text: What is the channel / sampling topology and application type?

single_or_multiplexed.label: Single-channel or multi-channel multiplexed (channels sampled sequentially)
single_or_multiplexed.desc: Simultaneous sampling is not required. Inter-channel time skew is acceptable for DC measurement, sensor reading, and non-phase-sensitive applications. Default weights apply for latency and power.

simultaneous.label: Multi-channel simultaneous sampling (all channels captured at identical moment — phase-sensitive)
simultaneous.desc: Simultaneous sampling escalated to mandatory + blockOnMissing. Multiplexed ADCs BLOCKED unconditionally — inter-channel time skew is fatal for phase-sensitive applications. Required for three-phase motor control, power quality metering, vibration analysis, cross-correlation measurement.

control_loop.label: Control loop / closed-loop feedback (fast response required)
control_loop.desc: Conversion latency escalated to mandatory + blockOnMissing. Delta-Sigma candidates flagged Application Review with calculated phase margin impact. Sample rate escalated to primary. Extra latency reduces phase margin: 1 ms latency @ 1 kHz control loop = −3.6° phase margin.

battery_powered.label: Battery-powered / power-constrained
battery_powered.desc: Power consumption escalated to primary. High-speed pipeline ADCs (100–500 mW) flagged Application Review for power. Delta-Sigma (1–50 mW) or low-rate SAR (<1 mW) preferred. Verify minimum sample rate still meets application requirements.

automotive.text: Handelt es sich um ein Automobildesign, das AEC-Q100 erfordert?

yes.label: Ja — Automobil (AEC-Q100 erforderlich)
yes.desc: AEC-Q100 becomes mandatory + blockOnMissing. Non-AEC-Q100 candidates removed from pool before scoring. Operating temperature range must cover −40°C to +125°C minimum. Required for ECU, ADAS sensor fusion, battery management systems, motor control ADCs.

no.label: Nein
no.desc: Standard-Qualifikationsabgleich. AEC-Q100 ist informativ — Vorhandensein ist informativ, aber nicht erforderlich.

operating_mode.text: Handelt es sich um eine Automobilanwendung?

saturated_switching.label: Saturated switching (digital logic driver, relay driver, solenoid driver, LED driver)
saturated_switching.desc: Storage time (tst) becomes the PRIMARY switching speed concern. Vce(sat) is the primary conduction loss spec. hFE need only be sufficient to saturate at required Ic — circuit designers typically overdrive the base by 5-10x minimum hFE.

linear_analog.label: Linear / analog (amplifier, buffer, linear regulator, current mirror, sensor interface)
linear_analog.desc: The transistor never saturates. hFE at the actual operating Ic and temperature becomes PRIMARY. ft determines bandwidth. SOA becomes critical at high Vce with significant Ic.

class_ab_pair.label: Class AB / push-pull output stage (audio amplifier, motor driver complementary pair)
class_ab_pair.desc: Both switching speed and analog performance matter. hFE matching between NPN and PNP halves is critical for symmetrical behavior. Vbe(on) matching determines crossover distortion. SOA required for quiescent operating point.

low_lt_10khz.label: Low frequency (<10kHz) — relay drivers, solenoid drivers, LED drivers
low_lt_10khz.desc: Storage time is a concern but not critical at low frequency — even a tst of 2µs is negligible at 1kHz. Focus on Vce(sat) and base drive adequacy.

medium_10k_100k.label: Medium frequency (10kHz–100kHz) — PWM motor control, power supply housekeeping
medium_10k_100k.desc: Storage time becomes a meaningful constraint. tst must fit within the off period. Anti-saturation techniques (Schottky clamp) become important. Verify tst at operating Ic and base drive conditions.

high_gt_100khz.label: High frequency (>100kHz) — high-speed logic drivers, switching regulators
high_gt_100khz.desc: Storage time is CRITICAL and likely the binding constraint. BLOCKING if tst is not specified in replacement datasheet. High-ft transistors designed for fast switching required.

complementary_pair.text: Is this a complementary pair application (NPN + PNP paired)?

yes_complementary.label: Yes — NPN and PNP are paired (push-pull, H-bridge, complementary symmetry)
yes_complementary.desc: Both NPN and PNP halves must be evaluated together as a matched pair. When possible, replace both with a known complementary pair (e.g., BC546/BC556, 2N3904/2N3906, 2SA1943/2SC5200).

no_single_device.label: No — single transistor, current mirror, or differential pair of same polarity
no_single_device.desc: Standard single-device substitution rules apply. For differential pairs (matched NPN-NPN or PNP-PNP), hFE matching between the two devices matters for offset voltage.

automotive.text: Handelt es sich um eine Automobilanwendung?

yes.label: Ja — Automobil (AEC-Q101 erforderlich)
yes.desc: AEC-Q101 qualification is mandatory. Operating temperature range must cover -40°C to +125°C minimum.

no.label: No — non-automotive
no.desc: Standard environmental matching applies.

driving_source.text: What is the logic family of the device driving the inputs?

ttl.label: TTL-output source (LS, ALS, AS, F, HCT, ACT, LVT)
ttl.desc: TTL-output families guarantee VOH_min = 2.4V — HC inputs (VIH = 3.5V at 5V) are in the undefined region. Only HCT/ACT inputs are safe.

cmos.label: CMOS-output source (HC, AC, LVC, AHC, ALVC, AUP)
cmos.desc: CMOS outputs swing rail-to-rail — compatible with both CMOS and TTL input thresholds at the same supply voltage.

mixed.label: Mixed or unknown driving sources
mixed.desc: Multiple driving sources or unknown — evaluate all interface constraints conservatively.

voltage_interface.text: Does this device interface between different voltage domains?

mixed_3v3_5v.label: Yes — 3.3V / 5V mixed interface
mixed_3v3_5v.desc: 3.3V and 5V devices on the same bus or signal path — requires 5V-tolerant inputs on 3.3V devices and level-compatible thresholds.

3v3_only.label: 3.3V only — all devices at 3.3V supply
3v3_only.desc: LVC, AHC, AHCT families required. TTL families (LS, ALS, AS, F) are BLOCKED — they require 5V minimum supply.

single_domain.label: 5V or other single voltage domain
single_domain.desc: All devices operate from the same supply voltage (5V or other) — standard compatibility rules apply.

bus_application.text: Is this device used in a shared bus or multi-driver application?

shared_bus.label: Yes — shared bus (I2C, SPI, parallel bus, backplane)
shared_bus.desc: Multiple devices share a common bus — output type and OE polarity mismatches cause bus contention.

point_to_point.label: No — point-to-point or single-driver
point_to_point.desc: Single driver, single receiver — standard output compatibility is sufficient.

input_signal_quality.text: Are the input signals slow-edged, noisy, or from analog/mechanical sources?

slow_noisy.label: Yes — slow edges, noise, or mechanical inputs
slow_noisy.desc: RC timing circuits, long PCB traces, crystal oscillators, switch debouncing, sensor inputs with slowly changing voltage.

clean_digital.label: No — clean digital signals with fast edges
clean_digital.desc: Standard digital signals from other logic ICs, MCU outputs, or properly terminated lines.

automotive.text: Handelt es sich um eine Automobilanwendung?

yes.label: Ja — Automobil (AEC-Q100 erforderlich)
yes.desc: Automotive electronics requiring AEC-Q100 qualification, extended temperature range, and reliability testing.

no.label: No — non-automotive
no.desc: Consumer, industrial, or commercial application.

dac_output_type.text: What is the DAC output type?

voltage_output.label: Voltage output (output pin produces a voltage proportional to digital code)
voltage_output.desc: Current-output DAC candidates BLOCKED unconditionally. Output voltage range becomes mandatory + blockOnMissing. Output buffered and output current source remain active for load drive capability assessment. Proceed to Q2 for resolution context, Q3 for application type.

current_output.label: Current output (sources/sinks current, typically 4–20 mA industrial loop)
current_output.desc: Voltage-output DAC candidates BLOCKED unconditionally. Output current source escalated to mandatory + blockOnMissing — full-scale current must match loop standard. Output voltage range and output buffered become not applicable. Verify output compliance voltage covers maximum loop resistance at full current.

precision_class.text: What is the resolution / precision class?

general_12bit.label: ≤12-bit general purpose (setpoint control, bias generation, non-precision)
general_12bit.desc: Default thresholds apply. INL ≤ 2 LSB and DNL ≤ 1 LSB are sufficient. Glitch energy and output noise density are Application Review. Reference type matching is standard.

precision_16bit.label: 12–16-bit precision (industrial process control, precision bias, test equipment)
precision_16bit.desc: INL, DNL, reference type, glitch energy, settling time, and output noise density all escalated to primary. INL tightened to ≤ 1 LSB, DNL to ≤ 0.5 LSB. At 16-bit resolution, linearity errors directly affect output voltage accuracy in precision applications.

high_precision_20bit.label: 16–20-bit high precision (metrology, calibration source, high-resolution control)
high_precision_20bit.desc: INL, DNL, glitch energy, settling time, and output noise density escalated to mandatory + blockOnMissing. Reference type escalated to mandatory. Reference voltage escalated to primary. At this resolution, linearity errors are catastrophic and reference accuracy dominates system performance.

audio.label: Audio (waveform reproduction, D/A conversion for speakers/headphones)
audio.desc: Glitch energy escalated to mandatory + blockOnMissing with threshold ≤ 10 nVs (professional audio). Update rate must match audio sample rate. Output noise density and settling time escalated to mandatory. Architecture escalated to primary (Delta-Sigma preferred for low glitch and noise floor).

precision_dc.label: Precision DC / waveform generation (calibration source, waveform generator, precision setpoint)
precision_dc.desc: INL and DNL escalated to mandatory (monotonicity required). Glitch energy and output noise density escalated to primary. Settling time escalated to primary. Reference type escalated to mandatory. Power-on reset state flagged as Application Review.

industrial_control.label: Industrial process control (control valve, actuator drive, 4–20 mA loop)
industrial_control.desc: Power-on reset state escalated to mandatory — verify output safe state on power-up before firmware initialization. Output buffered escalated to primary. Settling time escalated to primary for fast control loops.

battery_powered.label: Battery-powered / power-constrained
battery_powered.desc: Power consumption escalated to primary. High-speed or current-steering architecture candidates flagged Application Review for power budget. Verify DAC power-down mode current if system uses sleep/wake cycles.

automotive.text: Handelt es sich um ein Automobildesign, das AEC-Q100 erfordert?

yes.label: Ja — Automobil (AEC-Q100 erforderlich)
yes.desc: AEC-Q100 becomes mandatory + blockOnMissing. Non-AEC-Q100 candidates removed from pool before scoring. Operating temperature range must cover −40°C to +125°C minimum. Power-on reset state escalated to primary for safety-critical initialization.

no.label: Nein
no.desc: Standard-Qualifikationsabgleich. AEC-Q100 ist informativ — Vorhandensein ist informativ, aber nicht erforderlich.

driver_topology.text: What driver topology does this application use?

half_bridge.label: Half-bridge (high-side + low-side)
half_bridge.desc: SHOOT-THROUGH SAFETY: Output polarity, dead-time, and propagation delay become BLOCKING. All three must pass — any single failure causes shoot-through risk. Bootstrap diode also becomes critical for high-side supply.

full_bridge.label: Full-bridge (four switches)
full_bridge.desc: Same shoot-through safety requirements as half-bridge (two half-bridge legs). All three safety checks become BLOCKING.

single.label: Single low-side driver
single.desc: No dead-time or bootstrap concerns — single-channel buffers one gate signal. Dead-time and bootstrap rules suppressed.

dual_independent.label: Dual independent drivers
dual_independent.desc: Two independent gate drive channels — no inherent dead-time or shoot-through concern unless driving a half-bridge externally. Dead-time rules suppressed.

power_device_type.text: What type of power device does this gate driver control?

silicon_mosfet.label: Silicon MOSFET
silicon_mosfet.desc: Standard unipolar gate drive: Vgs = 10-15V on, 0V off. Most gate drivers are designed for this. No special voltage requirements.

igbt.label: IGBT
igbt.desc: Large gate charge (Qg up to 500nC+). Peak drive current becomes critical for acceptable switching speed and loss.

sic_mosfet.label: SiC MOSFET
sic_mosfet.desc: ENGINEERING REVIEW: SiC requires bipolar gate supply (-5V / +18-20V). Standard unipolar drivers cannot substitute. VDD range must support negative off-state voltage to prevent parasitic turn-on from Miller coupling.

gan_hemt.label: GaN HEMT
gan_hemt.desc: ENGINEERING REVIEW: GaN has a tight Vgs window (+5-6V on, 0V off). Overshoot above Vgs(max) ≈ 7V destroys the device. VDD accuracy and drive circuit become critical.

unknown.label: Unknown / not specified
unknown.desc: Power device type not known. Standard matching weights apply.

automotive.text: Is this an automotive application requiring AEC-Q100?

yes.label: Ja — Automobil (AEC-Q100 erforderlich)
yes.desc: AEC-Q100 becomes mandatory. Automotive gate drivers must handle load-dump, reverse polarity, and EMI (CISPR 25). ISO 26262 functional safety for ASIL-rated motor control may require preserved FAULT pin and diagnostic features.

no.label: Nein
no.desc: Standard qualification matching.

safety_isolation.text: Does this application require safety-rated galvanic isolation?

yes.label: Yes — safety isolation required (IEC 62368 / UL 508A)
yes.desc: Isolation type is already BLOCKING by default (identity w10 + blockOnMissing). This confirms the safety requirement and adds a review flag for isolation voltage verification.

no.label: No — non-isolated is acceptable
no.desc: No safety isolation requirement. Isolation type matching still applies (identity) but is about topology compatibility, not safety certification.

unknown.label: Unbekannt
unknown.desc: Safety isolation status not known. Conservative: isolation type remains BLOCKING by default.

high_frequency.text: Is the switching frequency greater than 200kHz?

yes.label: Yes — switching frequency > 200kHz
yes.desc: At high switching frequencies, gate driver power dissipation becomes critical: Pd = QG × VDD × fsw. Faster rise/fall times and lower propagation delay are needed to maintain efficiency and dead-time margin.

no.label: No — switching frequency ≤ 200kHz
no.desc: Standard frequency operation. Gate driver thermal dissipation is typically manageable. Standard matching weights apply.

unknown.label: Unbekannt
unknown.desc: Switching frequency not known. Standard matching weights apply.

switching_frequency.text: What is the IGBT switching frequency?

low_lt_20khz.label: Low (≤20kHz) — motor drives, UPS, welders
low_lt_20khz.desc: Conduction losses dominate at low switching frequencies. Vce(sat) is the binding specification — every 0.1V reduction at rated current saves watts directly. Switching losses (Eon + Eoff) are secondary. Most industrial IGBT applications fall here.

medium_20k_50k.label: Medium (20kHz–50kHz) — high-performance servo, solar inverters
medium_20k_50k.desc: Conduction and switching losses are comparable. Both Vce(sat) and Eoff matter. Field-Stop technology provides the best trade-off in this range.

high_50k_100k.label: High (50kHz–100kHz) — high-density PSU, induction heating
high_50k_100k.desc: Switching losses dominate. Eoff is THE critical parameter. Only Field-Stop IGBTs are viable — PT and NPT cannot switch fast enough. Consider whether SiC MOSFET is a better technology choice at these frequencies.

above_100khz.label: Above 100kHz — review recommended
above_100khz.desc: IGBTs are rarely used above 100kHz. SiC MOSFETs are almost certainly the correct technology choice at these frequencies due to zero tail current and dramatically lower switching losses. If an IGBT must be used, only the fastest Field-Stop types are viable.

switching_topology.text: Is this a hard-switching or soft-switching (resonant) application?

hard_switching.label: Hard switching (PWM inverter, motor drive, buck/boost)
hard_switching.desc: Both turn-on and turn-off occur with full bus voltage across the IGBT. Eon includes the opposing diode reverse recovery energy. Eoff includes tail current energy. Both contribute to total switching loss.

soft_switching.label: Soft switching / resonant (series-resonant, LLC, ZVS)
soft_switching.desc: Voltage swings to zero before the IGBT turns on (ZVS), eliminating turn-on loss. Turn-off still dissipates Eoff because collector current is non-zero at turn-off in most resonant topologies.

parallel_operation.text: Are multiple IGBTs operated in parallel for current sharing?

yes.label: Yes — parallel IGBTs
yes.desc: CRITICAL: Do NOT mix IGBT technologies in parallel. PT IGBTs have negative Vce(sat) temperature coefficient — the hotter device drops more current, heats further, and thermally runs away. FS/NPT have positive tempco (self-balancing). Mixing PT with FS/NPT causes the PT device to hog current. Vge(th) must be matched between parallel devices for equal dynamic current sharing.

no.label: No — single device
no.desc: Standard matching rules apply. Technology upgrade hierarchy (FS > NPT > PT) is valid.

short_circuit_protection.text: Does the application require short-circuit withstand capability?

yes_desat.label: Yes — desaturation detection (motor drive, traction, servo)
yes_desat.desc: BLOCKING: The gate driver uses desaturation detection to sense short-circuit events (Vce rises above threshold while gate is on). The IGBT must survive for tsc microseconds while the driver detects the fault and initiates controlled turn-off. If tsc of the replacement is shorter than driver response time (typically 5-10µs), the IGBT fails before protection acts.

no.label: No — no SC survivability required
no.desc: Application does not require short-circuit withstand (e.g., resonant converter with inherent current limiting, or external fast-acting fuse protection). Standard matching rules apply.

automotive.text: Is this an automotive or traction application?

yes.label: Yes — automotive / traction
yes.desc: AEC-Q101 becomes mandatory. Short-circuit withstand time is critical for traction inverter fault protection. Tj(max) of 175°C is typically required for traction applications in sealed enclosures with high ambient temperatures.

no.label: Nein
no.desc: Standard-Umgebungsabgleich.

rs485.label: RS-485 / RS-422 (differential transceiver — MAX485, ADM485, SN75176)
rs485.desc: RS-485 uses differential voltage signaling on A/B bus pairs with half-duplex (shared pair, DE/RE direction control) or full-duplex RS-422 (separate TX/RX pairs). Key specs: data rate (250 kbps slew-limited to 50 Mbit/s), bus fault protection (±15 V standard, ±60 V industrial), unit loads (network capacity), failsafe receiver (bus-idle detection), DE polarity, and slew rate class (EMI compliance). All CAN, I2C, and USB candidates are BLOCKED.

can.label: CAN / CAN FD (bus transceiver — TJA1042, MCP2551, SN65HVD230)
can.desc: CAN uses dominant/recessive arbitration on a single differential pair (CANH/CANL). Classical CAN: ≤1 Mbit/s, loop delay ≤255 ns. CAN FD: data phase ≤8 Mbit/s, loop delay ≤140 ns. Key specs: CAN variant (classical vs FD), TXD dominant timeout (bus-jamming protection), bus fault protection (±70 V automotive), standby current (ECU quiescent budget). All RS-485, I2C, and USB candidates are BLOCKED.

i2c.label: I2C / SMBus (bus buffer / isolator — PCA9600, P82B96, ISO1540)
i2c.desc: I2C uses open-drain SDA/SCL lines with external pull-ups. Speed grades: Standard 100 kbps, Fast 400 kbps, Fast-mode Plus (Fm+) 1 Mbit/s, High-speed (Hs) 3.4 Mbit/s. I2C bus buffers and isolators extend bus capacitance limits or provide galvanic isolation. Most RS-485 and CAN bus-level specs do not apply. All RS-485, CAN, and USB candidates are BLOCKED.

usb.label: USB (signal conditioning / ESD protection — TPD4S012, USBLC6)
usb.desc: USB interface ICs cover ESD/signal-conditioning devices on D+/D- lines. Speed grades: Full-Speed (FS) 12 Mbit/s, High-Speed (HS) 480 Mbit/s, SuperSpeed (SS) 5 Gbit/s. Most bus-level transceiver specs from RS-485 and CAN do not apply. All RS-485, CAN, and I2C candidates are BLOCKED.

isolated.label: Yes — isolated transceiver required (safety-rated system, ground-fault protection)
isolated.desc: Isolated RS-485/CAN/I2C transceivers integrate a galvanic isolation barrier with dedicated bus-side supply (VCC2). A non-isolated transceiver has no isolation barrier and no VCC2 — it cannot substitute for an isolated device in a safety-certified system. Both supply domains (host-side VCC1 and bus-side VCC2) must be verified. Isolation working voltage (VIORM) must cover fault-condition CM voltage.

non_isolated.label: No — non-isolated acceptable
non_isolated.desc: Standard non-isolated transceiver. Isolated replacements are technically acceptable as upgrades (superset capability) but may require dual-supply (VCC2) infrastructure that does not exist on a single-supply PCB.

industrial.label: Industrial field wiring (exposed connectors, long cable runs >100 m)
industrial.desc: Industrial environments with RS-485 in conduit, CAN in control panels, exposed connectors, and long cable runs demand higher bus fault protection (±60 V), ESD immunity (±8 kV IEC 61000-4-2 Level 4), extended CM range (−25 V to +25 V), EMI-compliant slew rate limiting, and higher differential output voltage for margin on long cables.

automotive.label: Automotive (ECU, BCM, gateway, underhood)
automotive.desc: Automotive environments require ISO 7637 compliance (load dump, supply transients), AEC-Q100 qualification (see Q4), and strict quiescent current budgets. CAN bus fault protection must be ≥±70 V for ISO 7637 load dump compliance.

consumer.label: Consumer / light industrial (protected environment, short cable <10 m)
consumer.desc: Protected environment with short cable runs. Standard parametric matching with minimum bus fault protection (±15 V) and reduced ESD requirement (±4 kV acceptable).

automotive.text: Is this an automotive design (AEC-Q100 required)?

yes.label: Ja — Automobil (AEC-Q100 erforderlich)
yes.desc: AEC-Q100 becomes mandatory. Non-automotive-qualified parts cannot substitute regardless of electrical match. Temperature range must cover −40°C to +125°C minimum (AEC-Q100 Grade 1). CAN transceivers in automotive ECU, BCM, and gateway designs are always AEC-Q100 qualified. RS-485 in automotive is less common but follows the same qualification requirements.

no.label: Nein
no.desc: Standard environmental matching. AEC-Q100 is operational — presence is informational but not required.

application_domain.text: What is the primary application domain for this JFET?

audio_low_frequency.label: Audio / low-frequency (preamplifier, microphone buffer, instrumentation)
audio_low_frequency.desc: 1/f noise corner frequency and noise figure at low frequencies are the binding specifications. Best audio JFETs (2SK170, IF3602) have fc of 10-100Hz. Unity-gain frequency (ft) and capacitances (Ciss, Crss) are irrelevant below 100kHz — corner frequencies from even 20pF Ciss are well above the audio band.

rf_vhf.label: RF / VHF low-noise amplifier (HF, VHF, UHF front end)
rf_vhf.desc: Unity-gain frequency (ft), input/output capacitances, and noise figure at the operating RF frequency are the binding specifications. NF rises sharply as frequency approaches ft. 1/f noise is irrelevant at MHz+ frequencies. Crss determines Miller-effect bandwidth limitation.

ultra_high_z.label: Ultra-high-impedance input (pH electrode, electrometer, ionization detector)
ultra_high_z.desc: BLOCKING: Gate leakage current (Igss) is THE binding specification. 10pA across 100G-ohm source impedance creates 1V offset error. Igss roughly doubles every 10C — must verify at maximum operating temperature. Applications: pH meters (10M-1G ohm), ion chambers, mass spectrometers, electret microphone capsules (1-10G ohm impedance).

general_purpose.label: General-purpose (switching, current source, VVR)
general_purpose.desc: Standard matching weights apply. No specific application domain drives parameter escalation. JFET may be used as constant-current source, voltage-variable resistor, or general-purpose analog switch.

matched_pair.text: Does the application require matched-pair JFETs?

yes.label: Yes — differential input or balanced circuit
yes.desc: Matched-pair JFETs are required for differential inputs (instrumentation amplifiers) and balanced preamplifiers. Single-device substitution rules are insufficient — must match Vp and Idss tightly between both devices. Some manufacturers bin into tighter tolerance grades (A, B, C suffixes). Cannot auto-approve without engineering verification of pair matching.

no.label: No — single device
no.desc: Standard single-device matching rules apply. Range overlap for Vp and Idss is sufficient.

automotive.text: Handelt es sich um eine Automobilanwendung?

yes.label: Ja — Automobil (AEC-Q101 erforderlich)
yes.desc: AEC-Q101 becomes mandatory. Automotive JFET applications include wheel speed sensor interfaces, pressure sensor front ends, and precision instrumentation. Temperature range: -40C to +125C. Must verify Igss at 125C (doubles every 10C from 25C spec), Vp temperature stability (~+2mV/C), and Idss temperature behavior for bias circuit robustness.

no.label: Nein
no.desc: Standard-Umgebungsabgleich.

output_cap_type.text: What type of output capacitor is used on the PCB?

ceramic.label: Ceramic (X5R / X7R / X6S / C0G)
ceramic.desc: CRITICAL: Ceramic capacitors have very low ESR (<10mΩ). ESR-stabilized LDOs will oscillate with ceramic output caps. The replacement MUST be ceramic-stable (internally compensated). If the replacement datasheet does not explicitly state ceramic stability, it must be rejected.

tantalum.label: Tantalum capacitor
tantalum.desc: Tantalum capacitors provide ESR in the 0.1–1Ω range. Both ESR-stabilized and ceramic-stable LDOs work correctly with tantalum caps. Standard matching rules apply.

electrolytic.label: Aluminum electrolytic capacitor
electrolytic.desc: Aluminum electrolytic capacitors provide ESR in the 0.1–5Ω range. Both ESR-stabilized and ceramic-stable LDOs work correctly. Standard matching rules apply.

unknown.label: Unknown / not specified
unknown.desc: Output capacitor type not known. Flag for engineering review — capacitor compatibility must be verified before approving the replacement.

battery_application.text: Is this a battery-powered or energy-harvesting application?

yes.label: Yes — battery or energy-harvest powered
yes.desc: Iq dominates the average current draw in sleep mode. A 50µA vs 1µA difference can mean 6-month vs 10-year battery life for coin-cell IoT sensors. Dropout voltage also becomes critical — lower dropout extends usable battery voltage range.

no.label: No — mains-powered or always-on high-load
no.desc: Iq is negligible compared to load current (e.g., 50µA Iq vs 500mA load = 0.01%). Standard Iq weight applies but is not the binding constraint.

noise_sensitive.text: Does the output supply a noise-sensitive analog circuit (ADC, DAC, RF, precision amplifier)?

yes.label: Yes — noise-sensitive analog load
yes.desc: PSRR, output voltage accuracy, and load regulation become critical. Supply noise directly corrupts signal quality in ADC/DAC/RF circuits. PSRR must be verified at the upstream switching frequency from the datasheet PSRR vs. frequency curve — the DC headline number is insufficient.

no.label: No — digital load or non-critical analog
no.desc: Standard PSRR and accuracy requirements. Digital loads (microcontrollers, FPGAs) are less sensitive to supply noise than precision analog circuits.

automotive.text: Handelt es sich um eine Automobilanwendung?

yes.label: Ja — Automobil (AEC-Q100 erforderlich)
yes.desc: AEC-Q100 becomes mandatory. Automotive LDOs must handle load-dump transients (40V on 12V systems per ISO 7637). Junction temperature rating must support the qualification grade: Grade 0 (−40 to +150°C Tj), Grade 1 (−40 to +125°C Tj).

no.label: Nein
no.desc: Standard-Umgebungsabgleich.

upstream_switching_freq.text: What is the upstream switching frequency (if post-regulating a switcher)?

none_dc.label: None — DC supply only (battery, linear pre-regulator)
none_dc.desc: No switching frequency to reject. PSRR at DC is relevant but typically excellent for all modern LDOs. PSRR is less critical in this configuration.

low_freq.label: Low (<500kHz) — typical buck/boost converters
low_freq.desc: Most LDOs have good PSRR (>40dB) below 500kHz. Verify from PSRR vs. frequency curve that the replacement maintains adequate rejection at the specific switching frequency.

high_freq.label: High (500kHz–2MHz) — high-frequency DC-DC
high_freq.desc: BLOCKING: PSRR degrades rapidly above 500kHz. Many LDOs have <20dB PSRR at 1MHz. Replacement must maintain adequate PSRR at the specific switching frequency — verify from datasheet PSRR vs. frequency curve. Missing frequency-specific PSRR data is unacceptable.

unknown.label: Unknown switching frequency
unknown.desc: Upstream switching frequency not known. PSRR stays as application_review — must be verified for the specific frequency before approval.

switching_topology.text: What switching topology does this MOSFET operate in?

hard_switching.label: Hard-switching PWM (buck, boost, half-bridge)
hard_switching.desc: Gate charge and Miller charge (Qgd) dominate switching losses. Minimize Qgd × Rds(on) product. Crss determines dV/dt noise injection.

soft_switching.label: Soft-switching / resonant (ZVS, LLC, CRM)
soft_switching.desc: Coss is part of the resonant tank — different Coss shifts resonant frequency and ZVS window. Qgd is less critical since drain swings to zero before turn-on.

linear_mode.label: Linear mode (hot-swap, eFuse, motor soft-start)
linear_mode.desc: MOSFET operates between fully-on and fully-off for extended periods. SOA curves are THE critical spec — must compare graphically. Vgs(th) determines operating point control.

dc_low_frequency.label: DC / low-frequency (load switch, ORing, battery protection)
dc_low_frequency.desc: Rds(on) dominates total losses — switching losses are negligible. Gate charge parameters are irrelevant.

synchronous_rectification.text: Is this MOSFET used in synchronous rectification?

yes_above_50khz.label: Yes — at ≥50kHz switching frequency
yes_above_50khz.desc: BLOCKING: Body diode trr is critical. During dead time, the body diode conducts. At ≥50kHz, high trr causes shoot-through — the complementary switch turns on before the body diode stops conducting, creating a momentary bus short circuit. Requires explicit engineering sign-off.

yes_below_50khz.label: Yes — below 50kHz switching frequency
yes_below_50khz.desc: Body diode trr matters but is less critical at lower frequencies. Dead-time losses are proportional to frequency.

no.label: Nein
no.desc: Body diode performance is secondary. Default matching rules apply.

parallel_operation.text: Are MOSFETs operated in parallel for current sharing?

yes.label: Yes — parallel MOSFETs
yes.desc: Vgs(th) has a negative temperature coefficient — the hotter device turns on first, carries more current, and heats further. Rds(on) positive tempco provides self-balancing in fully-on state, but Vgs(th) effect dominates during transitions. Use matched devices with individual gate resistors.

no.label: Nein
no.desc: Standard matching rules apply.

drive_voltage.text: What gate drive voltage does the circuit provide?

logic_level.label: Logic-level (3.3V or 5V)
logic_level.desc: Rds(on) must be specified at 4.5V Vgs (logic-level spec). A MOSFET specified only at 10V Vgs may have dramatically higher Rds(on) at 4.5V. Vgs(th) max must be well below drive voltage for full saturation.

standard.label: Standard (10V or 12V)
standard.desc: Default gate drive assumption. Most MOSFET datasheets specify Rds(on) at 10V Vgs.

high_voltage_sic.label: High-voltage SiC/GaN (15V-18V, with negative turn-off)
high_voltage_sic.desc: SiC/GaN gate drive uses +18V/−5V or +15V/−4V. The negative turn-off voltage prevents spurious turn-on from Crss coupling. Verify Vgs(max) covers both positive and negative excursions.

automotive.text: Handelt es sich um eine Automobilanwendung?

yes.label: Ja — Automobil
yes.desc: AEC-Q101 becomes mandatory. Avalanche energy is critical for UIS survival in automotive fault conditions. Operating temperature range must cover -40°C to +150°C.

no.label: Nein
no.desc: Standard-Umgebungsabgleich.

device_function.text: Handelt es sich um eine Automobilanwendung?

op_amp.label: Op-Amp (closed-loop feedback)
op_amp.desc: Amplifier operating in a closed-loop negative feedback configuration — signal conditioning, filtering, gain stages, voltage followers

comparator.label: Comparator (open-loop switching)
comparator.desc: Device operating open-loop for threshold detection, zero-crossing, PWM generation, or level shifting

instrumentation_amp.label: Instrumentation Amplifier (differential measurement)
instrumentation_amp.desc: Matched differential input stage for bridge sensors, biomedical, strain gauges — requires high CMRR

source_impedance.text: What is the typical source impedance driving the input?

low.label: Low impedance (< 1kΩ)
low.desc: Current sources, voltage dividers, low-impedance transducers — voltage noise dominates

medium.label: Medium impedance (1kΩ – 100kΩ)
medium.desc: Typical signal conditioning — both voltage and current noise contribute

high.label: High impedance (100kΩ – 10MΩ)
high.desc: Piezo sensors, pH probes, photodiodes, high-impedance dividers — current noise and bias current dominate

very_high.label: Very high impedance (> 10MΩ)
very_high.desc: Electrometers, charge amplifiers, glass-electrode pH — even pA-level JFET bias current may be too high; CMOS required

supply_configuration.text: Is this a single-supply or dual-supply circuit?

single_supply.label: Single-supply (e.g., 3.3V or 5V, ground-referenced)
single_supply.desc: Positive supply only, ground-referenced — VICM must include ground, output must swing near ground rail, RRO often required

dual_supply.label: Dual-supply (e.g., ±5V, ±12V, ±15V)
dual_supply.desc: Symmetric positive/negative supplies — standard VICM centered on ground, RR typically not needed

precision_application.text: What is the accuracy or noise requirement for this circuit?

precision_dc.label: Precision DC (instrumentation, 16-24 bit ADC, weighing)
precision_dc.desc: DC accuracy is paramount — Vos, Ib, Avol, and TCV are the dominant error sources. Autozero or chopper-stabilized types may be required.

low_noise_ac.label: Low-noise AC (audio, RF front end, sensor conditioning)
low_noise_ac.desc: Signal-frequency noise is paramount — en and in at the actual signal frequency determine SNR. 1/f corner frequency is critical for audio (below 1kHz).

general_purpose.label: General purpose (buffering, filtering, non-critical)
general_purpose.desc: Standard parametric matching — no special precision or noise escalation needed.

circuit_gain.text: What is the minimum closed-loop gain in your circuit?

unity.label: Unity gain (1 V/V)
unity.desc: Voltage follower / buffer — decompensated op-amps are BLOCKED (will oscillate)

low.label: Low gain (2 – 10 V/V)
low.desc: Low-gain amplification — some decompensated op-amps may be usable at gain ≥ 5

high.label: High gain (> 10 V/V)
high.desc: High-gain amplification — decompensated op-amps provide higher GBW at this gain

automotive.text: Handelt es sich um eine Automobilanwendung?

yes.label: Ja — Automobil (AEC-Q100 erforderlich)
yes.desc: Automotive electronics requiring AEC-Q100 qualification, extended temperature range, and reliability testing

no.label: No — non-automotive
no.desc: Consumer, industrial, or commercial application

switching_frequency.text: What is the switching frequency of this circuit?

mains_50_60hz.label: 50/60Hz (mains rectification)
mains_50_60hz.desc: trr is irrelevant — even a 5µs standard diode is negligible vs. the 16-20ms period. Vf dominates.

low_freq_1k_50k.label: 1kHz-50kHz (motor drives, low-frequency switching)
low_freq_1k_50k.desc: trr begins to matter. Fast recovery is the minimum. Soft/snappy recovery starts to matter.

smps_50k_500k.label: 50kHz-500kHz (SMPS, DC-DC converters)
smps_50k_500k.desc: trr and Qrr become primary — switching losses dominate. Ultrafast typically required.

above_500k.label: >500kHz
above_500k.desc: Consider Schottky or SiC diodes. Silicon rectifiers may be a legacy design at this frequency.

circuit_topology.text: What is the circuit topology or function of this diode?

power_supply_rectifier.label: Power supply rectifier (half/full-bridge, center-tap)
power_supply_rectifier.desc: Standard rectifier application. Io, Ifsm, and configuration are primary.

freewheeling_clamp.label: Freewheeling / clamp diode (inductor or relay coil)
freewheeling_clamp.desc: Fast/ultrafast recovery critical regardless of main circuit frequency. Reverse voltage must cover inductive spikes.

oring_redundant.label: OR-ing / redundant power
oring_redundant.desc: Vf matching between paths is critical. Reverse leakage matters (continuous reverse bias).

reverse_polarity.label: Reverse polarity protection
reverse_polarity.desc: Vf is critical (permanent voltage loss). Recovery time is irrelevant.

low_voltage.text: Is this a low-voltage application (supply voltage ≤12V)?

yes.label: Yes — supply ≤12V
yes.desc: Vf becomes the dominant concern. A 1.1V drop on 5V is 22% loss. Consider Schottky diodes.

no.label: No — supply >12V
no.desc: Standard Vf matching. A 100mV difference on 48V or 400V is negligible.

automotive.text: Handelt es sich um eine Automobilanwendung?

yes.label: Ja — Automobil
yes.desc: AEC-Q101 becomes mandatory. Note: Q101 for discretes, not Q200 (passives).

no.label: Nein
no.desc: Standard-Umgebungsabgleich.

low_voltage.text: Is this a low-voltage application (supply voltage ≤12V)?

yes.label: Yes — 3.3V, 5V, or 12V rail
yes.desc: Vf becomes the absolute dominant spec. Every 50mV matters. At 3.3V, a 0.45V Schottky drops 14% of the supply.

no.label: No — higher voltage (>12V)
no.desc: Vf is still important but less dominant. Reverse leakage becomes more significant (Ir × Vr = leakage power).

ambient_temperature.text: What is the operating or ambient temperature environment?

high_ambient.label: High ambient (>60°C) or poor thermal path
high_ambient.desc: Reverse leakage escalates to critical. Schottky Ir doubles every 10°C — thermal runaway risk is real.

room_temp.label: Room temperature / well-cooled
room_temp.desc: Standard Ir matching at 25°C is sufficient. Thermal runaway risk is low.

semiconductor_material.text: Is this a silicon or silicon carbide (SiC) Schottky diode?

silicon.label: Silicon (standard)
silicon.desc: Normal Schottky matching. Voltage ratings typically ≤200V. Vf is the dominant advantage.

sic.label: SiC (Silicon Carbide)
sic.desc: Different product category. 600–1700V. Higher Vf (1.2–1.7V) but zero recovery + high voltage + temperature stability.

unknown.label: Don\
unknown.desc: Determine from voltage: Vrrm ≥300V is almost certainly SiC. Below 200V is almost certainly silicon.

parallel_operation.text: Are diodes operating in parallel for higher current?

yes.label: Yes — paralleled for higher current
yes.desc: Vf temperature coefficient becomes critical. Positive tempco (high current) = safe sharing. Negative tempco (low current) = thermal runaway risk.

no.label: Nein
no.desc: Vf temperature coefficient is a secondary concern. Standard matching.

automotive.text: Handelt es sich um eine Automobilanwendung?

yes.label: Ja — Automobil
yes.desc: AEC-Q101 becomes mandatory. Note: Q101 for discretes, not Q200 (passives).

no.label: Nein
no.desc: Standard-Umgebungsabgleich.

architecture_type.text: Is this an integrated-switch converter or a controller-only design?

integrated_switch.label: Integrated switch (converter)
integrated_switch.desc: The IC includes the power MOSFET(s) on-chip. No external FETs on the PCB. Gate drive specifications are not applicable — the IC manages its own switch internally.

controller_only.label: Controller-only (external FETs)
controller_only.desc: The IC drives external MOSFETs via HG/LG gate driver outputs. Gate drive current and voltage become critical — they determine switching speed, efficiency, and EMI.

unknown.label: Unknown / not specified
unknown.desc: Architecture not known. Gate drive parameters will remain at default weight for conservative evaluation.

comp_redesign.text: Can the compensation network be redesigned, or must external components stay unchanged?

can_redesign.label: Compensation can be redesigned
can_redesign.desc: The COMP pin components (Type II/III network) can be changed. A control mode mismatch is still a significant concern (loop stability must be re-verified), but is not an automatic rejection — the compensation can be recalculated for the new IC\

cannot_change.label: Components must stay unchanged (drop-in only)
cannot_change.desc: No BOM changes allowed beyond the IC itself. Control mode must match exactly — the existing compensation network is tuned for the original IC\

unknown.label: Unbekannt
unknown.desc: Compensation flexibility not known. Conservative evaluation: control mode mismatch is a hard identity failure.

automotive.text: Is this an automotive application requiring AEC-Q100?

yes.label: Ja — Automobil (AEC-Q100 erforderlich)
yes.desc: AEC-Q100 becomes mandatory. Automotive switching regulators must handle load-dump transients (40V/400ms on 12V systems per ISO 7637) and be EMI characterized per CISPR 25. Tj(max) must support the qualification grade.

no.label: Nein
no.desc: Standard qualification matching.

passive_flexibility.text: Can the power inductor and output capacitors be changed, or must they stay as-is?

passives_can_change.label: Passives can be changed
passives_can_change.desc: Inductor and capacitor values can be adjusted for the replacement IC. Switching frequency deviation beyond ±10% is still flagged but not a hard rejection — passive values will be recalculated.

passives_fixed.label: Passives must stay unchanged
passives_fixed.desc: BLOCKING: The existing inductor and output capacitors cannot be changed. Switching frequency must match precisely — any deviation changes inductor ripple current, output voltage ripple, and potentially causes inductor saturation or instability.

unknown.label: Unbekannt
unknown.desc: Passive flexibility not known. Standard ±10% fsw tolerance applies.

high_conversion_ratio.text: Does this design have a high voltage conversion ratio (e.g., 12V→1V buck or 3.3V→24V boost)?

yes.label: Yes — high step-down or step-up ratio
yes.desc: BLOCKING: High conversion ratios push duty cycle to extremes. In a 12V→1V buck at 1MHz: D = 8.3%, ton = 83ns. If the replacement\

no.label: No — moderate ratio (e.g., 5V→3.3V)
no.desc: Moderate duty cycles leave adequate margin for ton_min/toff_min. Standard matching weight applies.

unknown.label: Unbekannt
unknown.desc: Conversion ratio not known. Ton_min remains at standard weight — review-on-missing behavior.

device_subtype.text: Um welchen Thyristortyp handelt es sich?

scr.label: SCR (Silicon Controlled Rectifier)
scr.desc: Unidirectional device — conducts anode-to-cathode only, triggered by positive gate pulse. Used in DC motor drives, crowbar protection, AC half-wave and full-wave phase control. Quadrant operation and snubberless rating do not apply.

triac.label: TRIAC (Triode AC Switch)
triac.desc: Bidirectional device — conducts in both directions, handles full AC cycle without bridge rectifier. Dominant in AC light dimmers, heating controllers, AC motor soft starters. tq (turn-off time) does not apply — TRIACs use natural AC commutation.

diac.label: DIAC (Diode AC Switch)
diac.desc: Two-terminal, no gate — triggers symmetrically when breakover voltage is reached. Almost exclusively used as a trigger device for TRIACs. No gate parameters, no quadrant operation, no tq, no snubberless rating.

application_type.text: Was ist die Hauptanwendung für diesen Thyristor?

ac_phase_control.label: AC phase control (dimmer, heater, fan speed)
ac_phase_control.desc: Standard AC phase-control application. Natural commutation at AC zero crossing provides ample reverse-bias time. Default matching rules apply — dV/dt and snubberless rating are the primary concerns.

crowbar_dc.label: Crowbar protection / DC chopper (forced commutation)
crowbar_dc.desc: CRITICAL: SCR forced-commutation application. The commutation circuit (capacitor + auxiliary SCR) is sized specifically around the main SCR tq. A longer tq replacement causes commutation failure (re-triggering, shoot-through, loss of control).

ac_zero_cross.label: AC zero-cross switching (solid-state relay)
ac_zero_cross.desc: Optocoupler-TRIAC combination for isolated AC switching at the zero crossing. Holding current matters because the device must sustain conduction through the full AC half-cycle, including the low-current region near the zero crossing.

motor_soft_start.label: AC motor soft-start / inrush limiting
motor_soft_start.desc: Motor startup currents are 6-10x rated for several seconds. Both surge current (ITSM) and I²t for fuse coordination become primary concerns.

snubber_circuit.text: Enthält das PCB-Design ein RC-Snubber-Netzwerk über dem Thyristor?

no_snubber.label: No snubber — snubberless device required
no_snubber.desc: BLOCKING: PCB has no snubber footprint. Only snubberless-rated TRIACs (high dV/dt immunity, 500-1000V/us) can be used. A standard TRIAC without a snubber will false-trigger from line transients — the circuit has no means to prevent it.

snubber_present.label: Yes — RC snubber present on PCB
snubber_present.desc: Snubber network limits dV/dt to the device rating. Standard or snubberless TRIACs can be used. Default matching rules apply.

automotive.text: Handelt es sich um eine Automobilanwendung?

yes.label: Ja — Automobil
yes.desc: AEC-Q101 becomes mandatory. Automotive thyristor applications include body electronics, HVAC control, battery management switching. Temperature range -40°C to +125°C minimum — cold-start IGT reliability is critical.

no.label: Nein
no.desc: Standard-Umgebungsabgleich.

555_timer.label: 555 / 556 Timer IC (NE555, TLC555, LM555, ICM7555)
555_timer.desc: All oscillator candidates blocked. 555 timers produce timing from external R and C — the IC does not contain a frequency reference. output_frequency_hz and output_signal_type are not applicable. Matching focuses on supply voltage range, timer variant (CMOS vs bipolar), output current, and quiescent current.

xo.label: Packaged Crystal Oscillator (XO)
xo.desc: All 555, TCXO, VCXO, OCXO candidates blocked unless stability class matches. Standard stability ±25–100 ppm over temperature. output_frequency_hz becomes mandatory + blockOnMissing. MEMS oscillators are accepted as valid cross-substitutions with Application Review flag.

mems.label: MEMS Oscillator (SiT8008, DSC1001, SiT8918)
mems.desc: Same matching rules as XO. MEMS replacements for crystal XO are valid drop-in substitutions if frequency, supply, and output type match — the one permitted cross-category substitution. Note MEMS-specific behavioral differences: faster startup (<1 ms vs 2–10 ms for crystal XO), lower current (1–5 mA vs 10–30 mA), better vibration/shock immunity, different phase noise signature.

tcxo.label: TCXO (Temperature Compensated Crystal Oscillator)
tcxo.desc: XO and OCXO candidates blocked (wrong stability class). TCXO stability (±0.5–5 ppm) is THE specification — it is why this class was selected. temp_stability_ppm escalated to mandatory. MEMS-TCXO variants are acceptable substitutions with Application Review.

vcxo.label: VCXO (Voltage Controlled Crystal Oscillator)
vcxo.desc: Fixed-frequency XO/TCXO candidates blocked — cannot replace a voltage-controlled device. vcxo_pull_range_ppm escalated to mandatory + blockOnMissing. Pull range must support the full frequency offset required by the PLL. Pull sensitivity (ppm/V) mismatch > 2× affects PLL loop dynamics — flag as Application Review.

ocxo.label: OCXO (Oven Controlled Crystal Oscillator)
ocxo.desc: All non-OCXO candidates blocked — TCXO cannot provide OCXO-class stability (±0.01–0.1 ppm). temp_stability_ppm and aging_ppm_per_year both escalated to mandatory. icc_active_ma escalated to primary (OCXO oven heater is the dominant power concern: 100–400 mW warm-up, 30–100 mW steady state). startup_time_ms escalated to primary (30 seconds to 5 minutes warm-up).

comms.label: Communications / protocol timing (Bluetooth, Wi-Fi, cellular, GPS, USB HS, Ethernet)
comms.desc: Protocol-specific frequency accuracy requirements. USB HS: ±100 ppm total; Bluetooth: ±20 ppm; Ethernet: ±100 ppm; cellular: ±0.1 ppm. initial_tolerance_ppm and temp_stability_ppm escalated to mandatory + blockOnMissing. Phase jitter escalated to primary.

precision.label: Precision / instrumentation (ADC sampling clock, metrology, calibration)
precision.desc: All frequency-related specifications become primary. Tolerance, stability, aging, and phase jitter are all important for precision measurement systems. ADC clock jitter directly limits achievable ENOB.

serdes.label: High-speed digital / SerDes (DDR memory, PCIe, SATA, USB HS)
serdes.desc: Phase jitter is the dominant specification. DDR3: ≤10 ps RMS; PCIe Gen1: ≤25 ps RMS; USB HS: ≤200 ps peak-to-peak. Duty cycle asymmetry closes the timing eye. Tolerance and stability are secondary — SerDes protocols tolerate ±100 ppm or more.

general.label: General digital / microcontroller clock (UART, SPI, I2C, general timing)
general.desc: Default weights apply. Tolerance and stability are secondary — UART/SPI/I2C protocols tolerate ±500 ppm or more. XO substitution for TCXO is acceptable with Application Review note.

battery_application.text: Handelt es sich um eine batteriebetriebene oder leistungsbeschränkte Anwendung?

yes.label: Ja — Batteriebetrieben, Energy Harvesting oder enges Leistungsbudget
yes.desc: Active current, standby current, and startup time all become primary specifications. For OCXO candidates: add review flag noting oven heater current (100–400 mW warm-up) may exceed the entire system power budget. Standby behavior matters: full shutdown (slow restart) vs output tri-state (fast restart).

no.label: Nein — Netzbetrieben oder Leistung nicht beschränkt
no.desc: Default weights apply. Active and standby current are secondary specifications.

automotive.text: Handelt es sich um ein Automobildesign, das AEC-Q100 erfordert?

yes.label: Ja — Automobil (AEC-Q100 erforderlich)
yes.desc: AEC-Q100 becomes mandatory. Non-automotive-qualified parts removed from candidate pool. Operating temperature range must cover −40°C to +125°C minimum (Grade 1). For MEMS oscillators: verify AEC-Q100 grade includes vibration characterization — not all AEC-Q100 MEMS oscillators are characterized for automotive vibration profiles.

no.label: Nein
no.desc: Standard-Qualifikationsabgleich. AEC-Q100 ist informativ — Vorhandensein ist informativ, aber nicht erforderlich.

tvs_application.text: Handelt es sich um eine Automobilanwendung?

power_rail.label: Power rail protection
power_rail.desc: Ppk and Ipp are PRIMARY. Clamping voltage must be below protected circuit\

signal_line.label: Signal-line protection (USB, HDMI, Ethernet, SPI, I2C)
signal_line.desc: Junction capacitance (Cj) becomes PRIMARY — the TVS loads the signal during normal operation. ESD rating becomes the primary power spec. Configuration/topology matters — steering diode arrays achieve lowest Cj.

automotive_bus.label: Automotive bus protection (CAN, LIN, FlexRay)
automotive_bus.desc: Must handle automotive transients (ISO 7637 load dump) while maintaining acceptable capacitance for bus speed. AEC-Q101 mandatory. Clamping voltage must meet bus transceiver maximum.

transient_source.text: What type of transient is this TVS protecting against?

esd.label: ESD (IEC 61000-4-2)
esd.desc: ESD rating is the primary power spec. Very short pulses (ns-scale), low energy (µJ–mJ). Response time must be <1ns.

power_surge.label: Lightning / power surge (IEC 61000-4-5, 8/20µs)
power_surge.desc: Ppk at 8/20µs waveform is the primary spec. Much higher energy than ESD. Cj is irrelevant.

telecom.label: Telecom lightning (GR-1089)
telecom.desc: Specific telecom surge standard. Very high energy. TVS must be explicitly rated for GR-1089.

automotive_transient.label: Automotive transients (ISO 7637 load dump)
automotive_transient.desc: Very high energy, long duration transients. TVS must be rated for automotive surge profiles.

interface_speed.text: What is the signal speed on the protected line?

high_speed.label: High-speed (USB 3.x, HDMI 2.x, PCIe, >1Gbps)
high_speed.desc: Cj must be ultra-low (<1pF per line). Steering diode topology preferred. Even 2–3pF causes signal integrity failures (eye diagram closure).

medium_speed.label: Medium-speed (USB 2.0, 100BASE-T Ethernet, SPI >10MHz)
medium_speed.desc: Cj should be <5pF per line. Standard low-cap TVS arrays work.

low_speed.label: Low-speed (I2C, UART, CAN, GPIO, <10MHz)
low_speed.desc: Cj up to 15–50pF is acceptable. More TVS options available. Power handling can be higher.

automotive.text: Handelt es sich um eine Automobilanwendung?

yes.label: Ja — Automobil
yes.desc: AEC-Q101 becomes mandatory. Must meet automotive transient profiles. Operating temp range must cover -40°C to +125°C or +150°C.

no.label: Nein
no.desc: Standard-Umgebungsabgleich.

configuration_type.text: Ist dieses Bauteil als Serienreferenz oder Shuntreferenz konfiguriert?

series.label: Series reference (dedicated Vout pin drives the load directly)
series.desc: Series references (REF50xx, ADR3x, LT6654, MAX60xx) have an internal error amplifier that actively regulates and drives the output pin. The device has separate IN, OUT, and GND pins. No external series resistor is needed. Shunt references are BLOCKED — they cannot drive the output and require an external resistor circuit that does not exist in a series topology.

shunt.label: Shunt reference (TL431 / LM4040 / LM385 — parallel with load, external series resistor)
shunt.desc: Shunt references sit in parallel with the load and regulate by sinking current through an external series resistor. The device has Anode, Cathode, and (for adjustable types) Reference pins. TL431 uses two programming resistors to set voltage. LM4040 is a 2-terminal device (like a precision Zener). Series references are BLOCKED — they would bypass the external resistor network.

fixed.label: Fixed voltage (1.2V, 2.048V, 2.500V, 3.000V, 4.096V, 5.000V, 10.000V)
fixed.desc: Fixed-output references produce a specific voltage determined internally. Standard reference voltages: 1.2V (band-gap), 2.048V and 4.096V (ADC-optimal — powers of 2), 2.500V (most common general purpose), 3.000V, 5.000V, 10.000V (metrology). Output voltage must match exactly — no tolerance allowed beyond the specified accuracy band.

adjustable.label: Adjustable — resistor-programmable (TL431 type) or trim-pin adjustable
adjustable.desc: Adjustable references use external resistors or a trim pin to set the output voltage. For TL431-type resistor-programmable references, the replacement\

high_precision.label: High precision (16+ bit ADC reference, metrology, calibration)
high_precision.desc: Initial accuracy (<0.05%), TC (<5 ppm/°C), 0.1–10 Hz noise, long-term stability, and architecture all become primary specifications. At 16-bit resolution, the difference between 0.05% and 0.1% initial accuracy = 32 LSBs of gain error. 0.1–10 Hz noise adds directly to ADC noise floor and cannot be averaged away. Buried Zener vs band-gap architecture determines noise floor and long-term drift class. NR pin usage must be preserved for noise filtering.

moderate.label: Moderate precision (12–16 bit ADC, sensor conditioning)
moderate.desc: Standard parametric matching with TC as a primary concern. Initial accuracy 0.1–0.2% is acceptable. TC 10–25 ppm/°C is acceptable for moderate temperature ranges. Output noise is important but typically adequate with modern band-gap references.

general_purpose.label: General purpose (comparator threshold, simple bias, non-precision)
general_purpose.desc: Relaxed matching — primary concerns are output voltage, dropout, and Iq. TC and noise are secondary. Architecture (band-gap vs buried Zener) is informational only — either works for non-precision applications.

automotive.text: Handelt es sich um eine Automobilanwendung?

yes.label: Ja — Automobil (AEC-Q100 erforderlich)
yes.desc: AEC-Q100 becomes mandatory. Non-automotive-qualified parts cannot substitute regardless of electrical match. Temperature range must cover −40°C to +125°C minimum (Grade 1). Automotive voltage references appear in BMS ADC references, motor control, and sensor interface circuits. Some automotive designs require documented long-term drift budgets for 10–15 year vehicle lifetimes.

no.label: Nein
no.desc: Standard environmental matching. AEC-Q100 is operational — presence is informational but not required.

zener_function.text: Handelt es sich um eine Automobilanwendung?

clamping.label: Voltage clamping / overvoltage protection
clamping.desc: Vz and power dissipation are the only primary specs. Tolerance can be loose (±5–10%). TC, Zzt, and noise are irrelevant.

reference.label: Voltage reference / precision bias
reference.desc: TC becomes primary — voltage stability is the whole point. Dynamic impedance (Zzt) and tolerance tighten. Noise may matter.

esd_protection.label: ESD protection on signal line
esd_protection.desc: Junction capacitance (Cj) becomes primary — high Cj degrades signal integrity on fast data lines. Reverse leakage matters.

level_shifting.label: Voltage level shifting
level_shifting.desc: Vz accuracy at actual operating current matters. Dynamic impedance determines level-shift variation with load. TC matters for temperature stability.

reference_precision.text: What voltage precision/stability is needed for this reference?

high.label: High precision (<0.1% stability over temp)
high.desc: TC ≤0.01%/°C, tolerance ≤1%, Zzt becomes a hard threshold. Consider dedicated IC voltage reference (LM4040, TL431) if requirements are very tight.

moderate.label: Moderate precision (0.1–1% stability)
moderate.desc: TC ≤0.05%/°C, tolerance ≤2%. Standard Zzt matching. Noise is secondary.

coarse.label: Coarse reference (>1% stability)
coarse.desc: Standard Vz and tolerance matching. TC and Zzt are secondary.

signal_speed.text: What is the signal speed on the protected line?

high_speed.label: High-speed digital (USB 2.0+, HDMI, SPI >10MHz)
high_speed.desc: Cj becomes a hard threshold — must be ≤ original. Consider dedicated ESD protection diodes (TVS arrays) instead.

low_speed.label: Low-speed digital or analog (I2C, UART, GPIO, sensors)
low_speed.desc: Cj is secondary — low-speed signals tolerate higher capacitance.

automotive.text: Handelt es sich um eine Automobilanwendung?

yes.label: Ja — Automobil
yes.desc: AEC-Q101 becomes mandatory. Note: Q101 for discretes, not Q200 (passives).

no.label: Nein
no.desc: Standard-Umgebungsabgleich.

reason.capacitance: A 100nF capacitor must be replaced by a 100nF capacitor. Ensure values are normalized before comparison.
reason.package_case: The replacement must match the original footprint exactly. MLCC pad geometries differ across sizes and are not interchangeable without a board redesign.
reason.voltage_rated: The replacement must handle at least the same voltage. Caution: higher voltage-rated MLCCs may exhibit different DC bias derating.
reason.dielectric: Dielectrics have a strict hierarchy. Class I (C0G/NP0) is most stable. You can upgrade (X5R→X7R) but never downgrade (C0G→X7R).
reason.tolerance: A tighter tolerance is always acceptable. ±5% can replace ±10%, but not vice versa. Common: ±20%(M), ±10%(K), ±5%(J), ±2%(G), ±1%(F).
reason.operating_temp: The replacement must cover at least the full operating range of the original.
reason.height: Critical for tight enclosures, stacked PCBs, and low-profile designs. A taller part may not fit.
reason.esr: Lower ESR is generally better for decoupling and filtering. Exception: some resonant/EMI filter designs require specific ESR.
reason.esl: Lower ESL improves high-frequency decoupling. Reverse-geometry packages have significantly lower ESL.
reason.flexible_termination: If original requires flex termination, replacement must also have it. Required on flex-rigid PCBs and large packages prone to flex cracking.
reason.msl: MSL 1 (unlimited floor life) is best. Lower MSL number is less restrictive and always acceptable.
reason.aec_q200: Required for automotive/high-reliability. A non-qualified part cannot replace a qualified one.
reason.dc_bias_derating: Class II MLCCs lose significant capacitance under DC bias. Different manufacturers can derate differently. Consult DC bias curves.
reason.packaging: Tape & Reel required for automated pick-and-place. Ensure reel width and pitch match feeder specs.

13.desc: Harte Logikfilter für die Validierung von Silberglimmer-Kondensatoren

reason.package_case: The replacement must match the original footprint exactly. MLCC pad geometries differ across sizes and are not interchangeable without a board redesign.
reason.voltage_rated: The replacement must handle at least the same voltage. Caution: higher voltage-rated MLCCs may exhibit different DC bias derating.
reason.dielectric: Silver Mica dielectric must match exactly. No Class I/II hierarchy applies — mica is a distinct material class.
reason.tolerance: Mica capacitors typically have very tight tolerances (≤1%). Precision is a primary reason for choosing mica. Tighter is always acceptable.
reason.operating_temp: The replacement must cover at least the full operating range of the original.
reason.height: Critical for tight enclosures, stacked PCBs, and low-profile designs. A taller part may not fit.
reason.esr: Lower ESR is generally better for decoupling and filtering. Exception: some resonant/EMI filter designs require specific ESR.
